about.title: GM Dashboard
about.blurb: Read the roster like a GM: who's worth their money, who to target by value, what rebuild phase you're in, and what just moved. Reads the same value spine the Coach dashboard reads -- value the coach loves is value the GM prices.

provenance.money_source: All salary and $/WAR figures are the CANONICAL 2026 base salary from the value spine (base salary known for a subset of ~180 roster spots; the rest show value-only). Full multi-year contract structure (years / guarantees / options / cap holds) is a v2 data acquisition, blocked on a Spotrac 403.
provenance.trade_value_note: The trade-value ranking is a BATCH/PREVIEW value engine (composite of projected WAR, age, and affordability) -- not yet a versioned production model. Its score orders the board; the money columns next to it are the canonical spine salary, not the engine's internal figure. The batch table itself still covers only 13 teams and predates the 2026 expansion; team-of-record is corrected from the live roster and the 2026 expansion clubs (POR/TOR) + unprojected newcomers that the batch table has no row for are SCORED at bake time with the engine's OWN reconstructed formula (projected WAR x contract-horizon x affordability) wherever the same reproducible inputs exist -- so their value is directly comparable to the ranked board, not a null gap. A newcomer with no 2026 projection (true rookie / no minutes) keeps an HONEST null (never a fabricated 0). This is a read-side reconstruction, NOT a write to the batch modeling table; a productionized, versioned trade-value table (real contract control years, filled salaries, native expansion scores) remains a follow-up data lane.
provenance.cap_space_note: No team cap-space / dead-money number is shown: it would require the full contract structure that is v2 data-gated. Total payroll from base salary alone (partial coverage) would mislead, so it is omitted, not invented.

trade_board.note: Players ranked by a batch trade-value engine (projected WAR + youth + affordability). Money columns are joined from the canonical spine, not the engine's internal salary. Team-of-record is the LIVE daily roster (all 15 teams incl. the 2026 expansion clubs POR/TOR, and each player's current team -- e.g. Angel Reese on ATL). Rostered players the batch engine has no row for are SCORED at bake time with the engine's own reconstructed formula wherever a 2026 WAR projection exists, using their REAL canonical 2026 salary rescaled into the engine's calibrated (2025-anchor) units for a salary-based affordability multiplier (a missing salary falls back to the engine's own null default), so their value is directly comparable; a newcomer with no projection (true rookie / no minutes) keeps an HONEST null -- never a fabricated 0, never silently dropped.

movement_wire.note: What just moved. Official signings/waives/trades, plus a media-rumor layer from social with a match-confidence score (lower confidence = less certain the report is matched to the right player). Availability is the dual-feed reconciled injury picture.

pick_value_curve.note: What a draft slot has historically returned (career WAR, peak WAR, seasons), league-wide and historical. This is NOT a claim about which picks any team currently owns -- it is a reference for valuing draft capital in the rebuild-vs-contend read.

deployment_watch.note: A scouting watch view, not a rating: three context reads on players whose raw numbers may misstate their real impact because of HOW they are deployed. Nothing here feeds a rating or a prediction; it flags film-room candidates.

usage_watch.note: Players used well below the typical share for their skillset role, yet efficient and delivering more impact than their workload predicts -- the 'badly used, not bad' candidates. Impact vs role expectation is net points per 100 possessions above what workload alone would predict.

context_watch.note: The same on-court impact, re-weighted so low-stakes blowout minutes count less. A positive shift = the raw scoreboard under-sells the player (her bad numbers came when the game was already decided); negative = raw numbers were padded in low-stakes time. For most players the shift is small -- these are the largest movers.

pairing_watch.note: Exploratory: an early league-wide read, not a proven effect and not a player rating. Which skillset pairings over- or under-perform the sum of their parts, in net points per 100 possessions vs the league pair baseline. Complementary spacing pairs tend to beat it; doubled-up on-ball creators tend to lose to it. League-level pattern only -- it says nothing about any specific duo.
